description.value:
When a creature that can see the [[lookup @name lowercase]]'s eyes starts its turn within 30 feet and the [[lookup @name lowercase]] isn't incapacitated, the [[lookup @name lowercase]] can force that creature to make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw.

[[/save con]]{Constitution Save}

  • Failure: The creature begins to turn to stone and is restrained. It must repeat the save at the end of its next turn, becoming petrified on a failure and ending the effect on a success.

  • Failure by 5 or more: The creature is instantly petrified.

Rolling the Eyes: Unless surprised, a creature can avert its eyes at the start of its turn to avoid the save, but it cannot look back at the [[lookup @name lowercase]] until the start of its next turn. If it glances at the [[lookup @name lowercase]] in the meantime, it must make the save immediately. The petrification lasts until the creature is freed by the @UUID[Compendium.elkan5e.elkan5e-spells.Item.Mi4qWwfmDYv6GrkI]{Greater Restoration} spell or similar magic.

Mirror Curse: If the [[lookup @name lowercase]] sees itself reflected on a polished surface within 30 feet while in bright light, it is affected by its own gaze.
